skill.name: prism-skill
skill.description: Multi-disciplinary dynamic analysis engine for Claude and Codex. Verifies facts, fills information gaps, and produces structured reports with tables, flowcharts, and exportable HTML.
skill.summary: A prompt-based analytical skill that adapts to any topic across disciplines, verifies claims with source priority, and outputs clear, structured, multilingual reports.
skill.author: sevenwoood

security_audit.summary: After evaluation, all 120 static findings are confirmed false positives. The skill is a pure prompt-based analysis framework with no executable code, scripts, or network calls. The 63 'external_commands' detections are markdown text-flow arrows (e.g., 'A → B') and installation shell snippets in README code blocks. The 30 'filesystem' detections are home-directory installation paths in comments (e.g., ~/.claude/skills/). The 12 'weak crypto' detections are text mentions of hashing or entropy in documentation. The 11 'high entropy' flags are natural multilingual prose (Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish). No malicious intent, exfiltration paths, or code execution vectors exist.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives: markdown text flagged as code execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 63 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across README, SKILL.md, and example files. All are markdown text-flow arrows (e.g., '美联储加息 → 美债收益率↑') or fenced code blocks showing benign installation commands (gh skill install, npx skills add, git clone). No actual code execution occurs.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives: installation paths flagged as hidden file access
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged references to ~/.cursor/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/, and ~/.codex/skills/ as 'hidden file in home directory.' These are documented installation target paths in README comments, not code that accesses hidden files.

content.user_title: Analyze Any Topic with Structured, Fact-Checked Reports
content.value_statement: Complex questions often cross disciplines and require verified evidence. Prism adapts to the right fields, checks facts against source priority, and delivers clear tables, flowcharts, and exportable HTML.
actual_capabilities: Dynamically selects 1-3 relevant academic disciplines for any input topic
Performs fact verification with source-level attribution when triggered
Fills missing information elements with clearly labeled inferences
Produces structured output including tables, text flowcharts, and lists
Generates self-contained interactive HTML reports on demand
Supports Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, and Spanish output
limitations: Does not provide investment, medical, legal, or engineering advice
Fact verification depth depends on training data and cannot access live sources
Cannot execute code, access networks, or read/write files at runtime
Complex analysis in non-Chinese/English languages may have reduced depth

use_cases.title: Economic Event Analysis
use_cases.description: A financial journalist asks how an interest rate hike affects ordinary people. Prism identifies economics and policy lenses, verifies the rate change with sources, and outputs a table of impacts across loans, savings, and employment.

use_cases.title: Social Issue Research
use_cases.description: A student researching corporate layoffs gets a multi-dimensional breakdown covering employment markets, psychological effects, and regional economic impact, with cited sources.

use_cases.title: Health Topic Overview
use_cases.description: A general user asks about a health topic. Prism presents evidence-graded information from authoritative medical sources, clearly marking predictions and unverified claims, while avoiding specific medical advice.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fact-Check Query
prompt_templates.prompt: I heard that [claim]. Can you verify if this is true? Please cite your sources.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner asking for a single-claim verification

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Disciplinary Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [event/topic] from economics, political science, and sociology perspectives. Show your reasoning in a table and add a causal flow diagram.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user wanting structured cross-domain analysis

prompt_templates.title: Report with HTML Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Give me a comprehensive analysis of [topic] with verified facts, then output the full report as an interactive HTML file with collapsible sections and dark mode.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requiring a polished exportable artifact

prompt_templates.title: Ethical Boundary Scenario
prompt_templates.prompt: Someone is asking me about [sensitive action]. How should I respond within ethical guidelines? Show the refusal pattern and alternative directions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user studying AI safety response patterns

output_examples.input: The Federal Reserve raised interest rates. How does this affect ordinary people? I want the real situation.
output: In short: rate hikes directly raise monthly payments on variable-rate loans and may curb price growth.
| Mortgage | ~140 yuan more per month on a 1M loan |
| Savings | Slight increase in deposit interest |
| Employment | Export sectors may face pressure |
Summary: the most direct impact is loan costs; other areas are less affected.
If you want to go further: tell me your specific loan situation, or how people in other countries are affected.

output_examples.input: A tech company laid off 30% of staff. Real case? Analyze from a social perspective.
output: Fact check: According to public reports, the company announced 30% layoffs in May 2026. Source: company press release and SEC filing. Verified.
- Job market: short-term supply increase, but high-skill workers typically re-employ within 3-6 months
- Psychological impact: laid-off workers may face anxiety; survivors often feel guilt and insecurity
- Regional economy: if the company is a major local employer, nearby businesses may dip short-term
Causal flow: Industry downturn + AI substitution → cost cutting → 30% layoffs → increased job competition → policy attention to retraining.

best_practices: Trigger the fact-check mode explicitly with keywords like 'real case' or 'fact check' when accuracy matters
Specify a discipline or perspective in the prompt to get more focused analysis
Request HTML export only after the full analysis is complete to get a coherent report
anti_patterns: Do not use Prism for actionable investment, medical, or legal recommendations
Do not feed private or sensitive personal data into analysis prompts
Do not expect real-time data access; Prism relies on training-time knowledge

faq.question: What tools does Prism work with?
faq.answer: Prism works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It is a prompt-based skill that runs inside the AI assistant.

faq.question: Does Prism access the internet or local files?
faq.answer: No. Prism is a pure prompt framework. It does not execute code, make network calls, or read files.

faq.question: Can Prism replace a professional advisor?
faq.answer: No. Prism is designed for analysis and explanation, not for actionable professional advice in finance, medicine, or law.

faq.question: Which languages are best supported?
faq.answer: Chinese and English receive the deepest analysis. Japanese, Korean, and Spanish are supported with slightly less depth in complex topics.

faq.question: How does fact-checking work?
faq.answer: When triggered, Prism verifies claims against a five-level source priority system, from academic journals down to logical inference, and labels unverified information.

faq.question: Can I export the analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. Request 'output html' to get a self-contained interactive HTML report with collapsible sections, dark mode, and one-click copy.
